documentation.description:
Universal Text File (UTF) format
A common format for Unicode text stored as lines

Uses at most 3 bytes per code point; ASCII codes appear as themselves
Common definition of a line across platforms
Any code point may appear in a line

tagged_types.name: File_Handle
tagged_types.code: type File_Handle is tagged limited private;

subtypes.name: Output_Mode
subtypes.code: subtype Output_Mode is File_Mode range Append .. Output;

subprograms.name: Create
subprograms.code: procedure Create (File : in out File_Handle; Name : in String; Mode : in Output_Mode := Output)

documentation.description:
If Name does not exist, creates Name for output
If Name exists, same as File.Open (Name => Name, Mode => Mode)
Raises Invalid_File if Name cannot be created, or exists but cannot be opened

subprograms.name: End_Of_File
subprograms.code: function End_Of_File (File : in File_Handle) return Boolean

documentation.description: Returns True if File is at the end of file; False otherwise

subprograms.name: Is_Open
subprograms.code: function Is_Open (File : in File_Handle) return Boolean

documentation.description: Returns True if File has been opened with Open or Create and not closed; False otherwise

subprograms.name: Mode
subprograms.code: function Mode (File : in File_Handle) return File_Mode

documentation.description: Returns the mode that File was created/opened with

subprograms.name: Next_Line
subprograms.code: function Next_Line (From : in out File_Handle) return Wide_Wide_String

documentation.description:
Gets a line from From
Raises EOF_Encountered if an attempt is made to read past the end of file

subprograms.name: Open
subprograms.code: procedure Open (File : in out File_Handle; Name : in String; Mode : in File_Mode := Input)

documentation.description:
Opens existing file Name in Mode:
   Append: open for output, positioned at the end of the existing file
   Output: open for output, truncating the existing file
   Input:  open for input, positioned at the beginning of the existing file
Raises Invalid_File if Name exists but cannot be opened in Mode

subprograms.name: Put_Line
subprograms.code: procedure Put_Line (To : in out File_Handle; Item : in Wide_Wide_String)
